questions.question: State what happens to the oxidation number of an element when it is oxidised.
Deduce the oxidation number of manganese in KMnO₄ and in MnO₂. Show your working for each.

Chlorine reacts with cold dilute sodium hydroxide to produce sodium chloride and sodium chlorate(I). Explain why this reaction is described as disproportionation. Use oxidation numbers assigned to chlorine in Cl₂, NaCl, and NaOCl to support your answer.
State the oxidation number of oxygen in hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂).
Deduce the oxidation number of sulfur in sulfur dioxide (SO₂) and in the sulfate ion (SO₄²⁻). Show your working for each.
In the reaction Fe₂O₃ + 3CO → 2Fe + 3CO₂, explain which species is oxidised and which is reduced. Use oxidation numbers to support your answer, showing the change for each relevant element.
State the oxidation number of fluorine in all its compounds.
Deduce the oxidising agent and the reducing agent in the reaction Zn + CuSO₄ → ZnSO₄ + Cu. Justify each choice using oxidation numbers.
Describe the rules used to assign oxidation numbers to elements in compounds and ions. Apply these rules to determine the oxidation number of nitrogen in (i) NH₃ and (ii) the nitrate ion, NO₃⁻.
Deduce the oxidation state of iron in iron(III) chloride (FeCl₃).
Deduce the oxidation number of chromium in the dichromate ion (Cr₂O₇²⁻) and in chromium(III) oxide (Cr₂O₃). Show your working for each.

Explain the meaning of the terms 'oxidising agent' and 'reducing agent' in terms of electron transfer and change in oxidation number. In the reaction 2Mg + O₂ → 2MgO, identify the oxidising agent and the reducing agent, with justification.
State the name of the type of reaction in which a single element is simultaneously oxidised and reduced.
Deduce the oxidation number of phosphorus in phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) and in the phosphide ion (P³⁻). Show your working for each.

Bromine reacts with hot concentrated sodium hydroxide to form sodium bromide and sodium bromate(V). Explain why this is a disproportionation reaction. Assign oxidation numbers to bromine in Br₂, NaBr, and NaBrO₃, and state which product results from oxidation and which from reduction.
